summary: Solana and Rust developer with about 8 years in software. The last 3 years I write production smart contracts on Solana with Rust, Anchor and the Solana SDK. Before that, my main stack was Node.js and React, and I'm open to backend Node.js roles. I connect on-chain programs to Node.js backends and web3 frontends, and I care a lot about secure code: Ed25519 signature checks, replay protection, and spotting scams and malicious code before they ship. I can take a project end to end — contracts, backend and frontend.

bullets: Built a compressed NFT (cNFT) system on Solana state compression: minting, storage and on-chain verification.
Designed it to keep costs low when minting NFTs at large scale.
Wrote the smart contracts for a token launchpad: fair launches, automated liquidity locking, whitelists, and migration to Raydium.
Built a DEX integration SDK to trade across Raydium, Pump.fun, Moonshot, Orca and Jupiter from one interface.
Built the onboarding frontend so teams could create and launch their tokens themselves.
Wrote, tested and deployed Solana programs in Rust with the Solana SDK and Anchor.
Added Ed25519 signature verification and replay protection to keep on-chain actions safe from forged or repeated transactions.
Maintained the Node.js backend that bridges chain and server: wallet calls, transaction processing, and on-chain data sync.
Started on the Node.js backend, then moved to Rust and Solana work across the full Web3 stack (Anchor, web3.js, TypeScript).
Built an on-chain betting game in Rust with reward distribution handled fully on-chain.
Built the APIs and database for a large crypto-swap portal.
Used RabbitMQ for async jobs, Redis for cache, and Jest for tests; integrated with smart contracts.
Built backend services for an NFT-sales startup, with real-time features over WebSocket and SSE.
Worked with PostgreSQL, Redis, a microservice setup, and third-party integrations.
Built React and Vue interfaces with Redux and backend API integration. Moved into web3 development here.
Built SPAs, started Node.js backend work, and grew into a team lead role (planning, architecture, code reviews).
Turned designs into working website frontends — HTML/CSS markup plus JavaScript for the interactive parts. First dev job and the start of the whole path.
